stem: A factory produces automotive parts using two sources. Robot Arm Station 1 handles 54% of production with a known defect rate of 2.7%. Robot Arm Station 2 handles 46% of production, but its defect rate is uncertain — recent quality audits suggest it lies between 3.2% and 6.6%, depending on raw material batch quality. A randomly selected automotive part is found to be defective. What is the probability it came from Robot Arm Station 2?

ground_truth.answer: P(Robot Arm Station 2 | defective) ranges from 0.502 to 0.676 as Robot Arm Station 2's defect rate varies from 3.2% to 6.6%. Point estimate at 4.9%: 0.607.

solution_steps: Apply Bayes' theorem: P(B|def) = P(def|B)×P(B) / [P(def|A)×P(A) + P(def|B)×P(B)].
The interval [0.502, 0.676] captures the range of posterior probabilities.
common_failure_modes: Using only the midpoint defect rate and providing a single answer
Comparing defect rates directly without applying Bayes' theorem
